reviews.review_description: I was expecting there to be actual gameplay in this game, but it's more just like a funny movie that lasts for about 45mins. Good acting as expected. Cute animations. But not a lot of substance given the short length and lack of interaction. If you're interested, wait for a sale...

A delightful, funny, and emotionally rich VR experience, carried by superb performances. The Doyle-Chet duo, portrayed with finesse by Jason Isaacs and Haley Joel Osment, evolves over nearly 800 years, blending poetry, humor, and tenderness.
You truly feel an affection for these characters, trapped in stone yet so vividly alive.
The technical execution is flawless, the sound is spatially immersive, and the writing is clever.
A success in every aspect, both in VR and MR modes.
This is the kind of experience I love to share with my friends.
The dialogue is delightful, as are the animations.
Subtitles in multiple languages ​​(including French) make it accessible to everyone.
With the two films, AJAX and GARGOYLE DOYLE, we see Ethan Shaftel's full talent, and we can only regret that there aren't more titles of this quality. A must-see.
Gameplay/Options
This is a narrative experience (360° VR film) of approximately 40 minutes with some hand-tracking interactions. The room-scale MR mode (museum) is an option that adds value, though it's not essential. The choice of subtitles in multiple languages ​​and the ability to select chapters are additional features that enhance the experience.

reviews.review_title: A Must Experience VR animated short
reviews.review_description: Question: Why did Karl Marx dislike Earl Gray Tea so much? Answer: Because all proper tea is theft.

This is very entertaining. You can set this up in a big room and do mixed reality. If you have the space it’s very cool, but mostly you are sitting anyway, so you the seated version in the app is just as good.
It was like a real movie in VR. a comedy drama about friendship. acting was great, worth watching. if you like this one, look up Ajax All Powerful by the same company.
Don’t want to give spoilers but the ending was not what I expected. There’s some trippy surreal stuff going on in this.
Thoroughly enjoyed it! Entertaining, illuminating, pulling a bit on the heart strings, very funny!

One of the aspects of a good film for me is when you can learn something from what you see. I put myself in Doyle's shoes and feel that sometimes the people around you can bother you and you want to be alone, but without those people you feel so alone and you have to appreciate the people who are always with you no matter what. Great story!

On the Quest 3 the graphics are significantly downgraded from what’s shown in the store trailer.  Muddy textures, no real time shadows, flat lighting, etc. The Quest 3 is capable of better than this.

The experience is like watching a trailer for a Pixar film and after you arrive at the theater, paid for your ticket, and settled down with your popcorn and drink, a movie rendered on a PlayStation 2 appears on the screen.

I will give it two stars though for the character animation, that’s really good.

Gargoyle Doyle is one of those rare experiences that isn’t a video game but still pulled me in. The light and observational heart of humanity was so engaging, and the light interactivity where I moved the story along felt way more immersive than sitting down and watching a typical film on the tv. 

I had a few laughs and really enjoyed the little bronze companion with Doyle. 

I think this type of thing is a great story to show family who haven’t tried VR media yet or only played one of those games like bear saber. Hope to see more ideas like this!
Overall, very cool animated story. Definitely recommend. Although there's no MR to be had. I guess Apple Vision Pro users got that part of the deal.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback! We’re glad to hear you enjoyed Gargoyle Doyle. We’re currently working on an update that will include the full mixed-reality version that toured film festivals, as well as a seated version. We’ll let you know as soon as it’s available!
- Gargoyle Doyle's team

reviews.review_description:
Very fun characters, I love the voice actors and dialogs give something real to status and gargoyles.
Even if the app is not a game I had a very good time with this.
If Pixar did VR it would be this. A must have. You’ll want your friends and family to experience this also.
